concerns.text: FR-011 and User Story 3 require a 'human-verified subset' to validate label noise. Tasks T035-T037 implement a 'random label flip noise model' (synthetic simulation) instead of a task for actual human review or integration of human labels. The requirement for human verification is unfulfilled.

FR-004 and User Story 2 mandate 'embedding-based similarity to known vulnerable patterns (using a pre-trained code encoder)'. Tasks T018-T020 cover structural and semantic metrics, but no task explicitly implements loading a pre-trained code encoder or computing the embedding similarity vector.

FR-002 specifies 'batches of ≤50 samples'. Task T013 and T015 implement 'batch size 10' as a plan override. While the plan notes this override, the tasks themselves do not include a mechanism to dynamically adjust batch size or a task to validate the memory constraint with the reduced batch size, leaving the spec requirement technically unmet by the current task set.

SC-002 defines success as 'adjusted R² > 0.10' for a linear model. Task T030 implements Logistic Regression (per plan override) which uses 'Pseudo-R²'. There is no task to update the success criterion check or verify Pseudo-R² > 0.10, making SC-002 untestable as written against the implemented logic.

Task T013 hardcodes the model to 'microsoft/Phi-mini-4k-instruct'. The spec (Assumptions) lists multiple candidate models (CodeLlamaB, StarCoder-Base, distilled Llama-2) without specifying a single default. A task to select or configure the model from the candidate list is missing, restricting the experiment scope.
